pages.title: The Arsenal's masonry
pages.text: Most of the Arsenal's appeal is functional, but a handful of blocks exist purely to dress a blood-mage's workshop. All are mined with any tool and keep their block-item form on drop.

pages.title: Blood-Infused Wood
pages.text: A full wood family: $(item)planks$(), $(item)log$(), $(item)stairs$(), $(item)slab$(), $(item)fence$(), $(item)fence gate$(). Crimson-hued and flammable — handle accordingly.

pages.title: Blood-Stained Glass
pages.text: Translucent, spawn-proof, non-conductive. Breaking it yields $(item)Glass Shards$() — feed those into the $(l:bloodmagic:soa_additions_ba/glass)glass daggers$() chain.

pages.title: Blood-Stained Glass Pane
pages.text: The pane form — connects to neighbors like an iron bar. Cheap to produce from full blocks via vanilla cutting.

pages.title: Blood Torch
pages.text: Brighter than vanilla torch by a notch. Light level 14 on wall or floor.

pages.title: Blood-Infused Glowstone
pages.text: Full-block light source at level 15. Pairs nicely with $(item)Blood-Stained Glass$() for stained-ceiling lighting.

pages.title: Blood-Burned String
pages.text: A tripwire variant. Connects to $(item)Tripwire Hooks$() and all standard redstone-line uses — $(italic)without$() the attrition from lava or falling blocks that burns vanilla string.

pages.title: Slate Block
pages.text: Condensed storage: nine of a single $(item)Blood Magic Slate$() (blank, reinforced, imbued, demonic, or ethereal) craft one block, and one block decompresses back. The block itself carries no tier — it's a storage form only, chosen by which slate goes in.
